news.summary: Many residents of Tehran managed to get out of town when the U.S. and Israel attack began, but others who could not described living under bombardment.

news.headline: Kabul rocked by blasts as Afghan, Pakistani troops intensify border clashes
news.summary: AFP journalists in Kabul heard multiple explosions and gunfire on Tuesday as Afghan and Pakistani troops continued border clashes. news.headline: When Trump Meets Germany’s Merz, Iran Will Likely Dominate Discussion
news.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz’s visit to Washington, long planned, is now likely to be dominated by discussions of the attack, which Europeans did not take part in.

news.headline: U.S. Hunts for Militants as the Nigerian Military Is Accused of Inaction
news.summary: The country has one of the most feared armed forces in Africa, yet within its own borders, it has repeatedly failed to stop abductions and attacks.

news.headline: Generals stripped of CPPCC roles as China’s corruption clean-up continues
news.summary:
China has revoked the political advisory seats of three generals, including two who previously held key positions in the People’s Liberation Army (PLA) ground force, as the anti-corruption drive continues within the top brass and defence industry.
